name: Miyagi Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the rich greenery of Miyagi's plains and mountains
the bush clover (hagi), Miyagi's prefectural flower, and the snows of the highlands

description: A deep green field charged at the centre with a stylised white emblem suggesting a bush clover (hagi) blossom and incorporating the katakana for 'Miyagi'.
history: Miyagi's prefectural symbol, designed by Suzuki Hisashi, was adopted on 22 April 1966 and the flag bearing it on a green field is in use from the same date.

features.symbolism: the verdant rice fields and forests of Miyagi

features.role: stylised bush clover (hagi), Miyagi's prefectural flower, with the katakana 'mi' (ミ) implied by the form
features.symbolism: the hagi flower, official prefectural flower of Miyagi; the design also incorporates the katakana letters 'mi' (ミ) and the kana 'ya' (ヤ) for Miyagi.
